volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 4.5.774c - Section 4.7.840)

sections.label: Section 4.5.774c
sections.translation: The same ;

sections.label: Section 4.5.775
sections.translation: and , in fact , he ’ ll request that she may be asked for .

sections.label: Section 4.5.775b
sections.translation: For what purpose , Syrus ? For I don ’ t altogether comprehend it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.776b
sections.translation: O dear , you are so dull .

sections.label: Section 4.5.777
sections.translation: Perhaps so .

sections.label: Section 4.5.777b
sections.translation: Money will be given him for the wedding — with which golden trinkets and clothes — do you understand me ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.778b
sections.translation: To buy them —?

sections.label: Section 4.5.779
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 4.5.779b
sections.original: At ego illi neque do , neque despondeo .
sections.translation: But I neither give nor betroth my daughter to him .

sections.label: Section 4.5.780
sections.translation: But why ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.780b
sections.original: Quamobrem ? me rogas ? Homini fugitivo dabo ?
sections.translation: Why , do you ask me ? To a fellow —

sections.label: Section 4.5.781
sections.original: Non ego dicebam in perpetuum illam illi ut dares ;
sections.translation: Just as you please . I don ’ t mean that in reality you should give her to him , but that you should pretend it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.782b
sections.translation: Pretending is not in my way ; do you mix up these plots of yours , so as not to mix me up in them . Do you think that I ’ ll betroth my daughter to a person to whom I will not marry her ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.785
sections.translation: I imagined so .

sections.label: Section 4.5.785b
sections.translation: By no means .

sections.label: Section 4.5.785c
sections.translation: It might have been cleverly managed ; and I under-took this affair for the very reason , that a short time since you so urgently requested it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.787b
sections.translation: I believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.787c
sections.translation: But for my part , Chremes , I take it well and good , either way .

sections.label: Section 4.5.788b
sections.translation: But still , I especially wish you to do your best for it to be brought about ; but in some other way .

sections.label: Section 4.5.790
sections.original: Fiat : quaeratur aliud : sed illud quod tibi
sections.translation: It shall be done : some other method must be thought of ; but as to what I was telling you of ,— about the money which she owes to Bacchis ,— that must now be repaid her . And you will not , of course , now be having recourse to this method ; What have I to do with it ? Was it lent to me ? Did I give any orders ? Had she the power to pawn my daughter

sections.label: Section 4.5.795
sections.original: Meam , me invito , potuit ? Verum illud , Chreme ,
sections.translation: without my consent ? They quote that saying , Chremes , with good reason , Riorous law [*] is often rigorous injustice .

sections.label: Section 4.5.797
sections.translation: I will not do so .

sections.label: Section 4.5.797b
sections.original: Immo aliis si licet , tibi non licet .
sections.translation: On the contrary , though others were at liberty , you are not at liberty ; all think that you are in good and very easy circumstances .

sections.label: Section 4.5.799
sections.original: Quin egomet iam ad eam deferam .
sections.translation: Nay rather , I ’ ll at once carry it to her myself .

sections.label: Section 4.5.799b
sections.translation: Why no ; request your son in preference .

sections.label: Section 4.5.800b
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.800c
sections.original: Quia enim in hunc suspicio est
sections.translation: Why , because the suspicion of being in love with her has been transferred to him with Menedemus .

sections.label: Section 4.5.801b
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.801c
sections.translation: Because it will seem to be more like probability when he gives it her ; and at the same time I shall effect more easily what I wish . Here he comes too ; go , and bring out the money .

sections.label: Section 4.5.804b
sections.translation: I ’ ll bring it .

sections.label: Section 4.6.805
sections.original: Nulla est tam facilis res quin difficilis siet ,
sections.translation: There is nothing so easy but that it becomes difficult when you do it with reluctance . As this walk of mine , for instance , though not fatiguing , it has reduced me to weariness . And now I dread nothing more than that I should be packed off somewhere hence once again , that I may not have access to Bacchis .

sections.label: Section 4.6.810
sections.original: Ut te quidem omnes Dii Deaeque , quantum est , Syre ,
sections.translation: May then all the Gods and Goddesses , as many as exist , confound you , Syrus , with these stratagems and plots of yours . You are always devising something of this kind , by means of which to torture me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.813b
sections.original: I tu hinc quo dignus es .
sections.translation: Will you not away with you — to where you deserve ? How nearly had your forwardness proved my ruin !

sections.label: Section 4.6.815
sections.original: Vellem hercle factum : ita meritus es .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I wish it had been so ; just what you deserve .

sections.label: Section 4.6.815b
sections.translation: Deserve ? How so ? Really , I ’ m glad that I ’ ve heard this from you before you had the money which I was just going to give you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.818
sections.original: Quid igitur dicam tibi vis ? abiisti , mihi
sections.translation: What then would you have me to say to you ? You ’ ve made a fool of me ; brought my mistress hither , whom I ’ m not allowed to touch —

sections.label: Section 4.6.820
sections.original: Iam non sum iratus : sed scin ubi nunc sit tibi
sections.translation: Well , I ’ m not angry then . But do you know where Bacchis is just now ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.821b
sections.translation: At our house .

sections.label: Section 4.6.821c
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 4.6.821d
sections.translation: Where then ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.821e
sections.translation: At Clinia ’ s .

sections.label: Section 4.6.822
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined !

sections.label: Section 4.6.822b
sections.original: Bono animo es : iam argentum ad eam deferes
sections.translation: Be of good heart ; you shall presently carry to her the money that you promised her .

sections.label: Section 4.6.823b
sections.translation: You do prate away .— Where from ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.823c
sections.translation: From your own father .

sections.label: Section 4.6.824
sections.translation: Perhaps you are joking with me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.824b
sections.translation: The thing itself will prove it .

sections.label: Section 4.6.825
sections.original: Nae ego fortunatus homo sum : deamo te , Syre .
sections.translation: Indeed , then , I am a lucky man . Syrus , I do love you from my heart .

sections.label: Section 4.6.826
sections.original: Sed pater egreditur : cave quicquam admiratus sies
sections.translation: But your father ’ s coming out . Take care not to express surprise at any thing , for what reason it is done ; give way at the proper moment ; do what he orders , and say but little .

sections.label: Section 4.7.829
sections.translation: Where ’ s Clitipho now ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.829b
sections.translation: Say — here I am .

sections.label: Section 4.7.829c
sections.translation: Here am I .

sections.label: Section 4.7.830
sections.translation: Have you told him how it is ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.830b
sections.translation: I ’ ve told him pretty well every thing .

sections.label: Section 4.7.831
sections.translation: Take this money , and carry it .

sections.label: Section 4.7.831b
sections.translation: Go — why do you stand still , you stone ; why don ’ t you take it ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.832b
sections.translation: Very well , give it me .

sections.label: Section 4.7.832c
sections.translation: Follow me this way directly . ( To CHREMES .) You in the mean while will wait here for us till we return ; for there ’ s no occasion for us to stay there long .

sections.label: Section 4.7.835
sections.original: Minas quidem iam decem habet a me filia ,
sections.translation: My daughter , in fact , has now had ten minae from me , which I consider as paid for her board ; another ten will follow these for clothes ; and then she will require two talents for her portion . How many things , both just and unjust , are sanctioned by custom ! [*]

sections.label: Section 4.7.840
sections.original: Mihi nunc , relictis rebus , inveniendus est
sections.translation: Now I ’ m obliged , neglecting my business , to look out for some one on whom to bestow my property , that has been acquired by my labor .
